mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (Spearman rho < 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, monetary policy is tight, raising discount rates and borrowing costs, which depresses equity valuations and corporate profits, causing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, forward 60-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (mean < 0, one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated retail sales and industrial production indicate robust domestic demand without supply constraints, boosting earnings expectations and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term RSAFS*INDPRO in a linear regression with forward 20-day SPX returns has p-value > 0.05 or out-of-sample Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a very low and still declining unemployment rate signals overheating and impending recession, causing negative forward returns as markets anticipate tightening and economic slowdown.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4% and has declined over the prior 3 months, forward 20-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (mean < -0.01, t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million units signal strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which boosts cyclical equities and drives short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the mean forward 5-day SPX return is not significantly above zero (p > 0.10, one-sided t-test).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises relative to CPI inflation, real interest rates increase, tightening financial conditions and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The spread (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL year-over-year change) does not predict forward 60-day SPX returns with a Spearman Rank IC above 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth reflects overall economic expansion, driving corporate earnings higher and supporting a positive trend in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 year-over-year growth does not show a positive Pearson correlation (r < 0.1) with forward 60-day SPX returns over rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals the onset of a recessionary contraction, causing negative forward returns as markets price in falling output and earnings.
failure_condition: When INDPRO MoM declines by more than 0.5%, the mean forward 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05, one-sided t-test vs zero).
